response:
to find the answer we must store the prime factors of one hundred factorial.

the power of two in one hundred factorial is much larger than fifty.

we must store the count of prime five in the factorization process.

there are twenty multiples of five in the numbers from one to one hundred.

add the extra factors from multiples of twenty five to store the total.

this gives us twenty five factors of five in the numerator value.

the denominator is a power of ten which equals two times five.

we need to store the remaining factors of five after division.

the exponent of five in the denominator will be fifty minus twenty five.

so the final denominator is two to the power of twenty five.
